FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Fintech and big tech push into banking regulation while regional M&A percolates and Fed hawks dig in on rates.

bullets.text: Klarna files for ILC charter to become deposit-taking lender under FDIC supervision, escalating fintech's push into traditional banking.
Sony gets conditional OCC nod for trust charter to launch stablecoin platform Connectia in 2027—major tech player entering regulated banking infrastructure.
Regional deals heating up: Bank7 eyeing 71% of Century Bank via forced sale after shareholder default; Hometown Bank buying NH peer for $160M.
Fed minutes will expose sharp internal divide on rate cuts—hawk-dove split could stretch debate across multiple meetings, keeping policy path uncertain.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: RIA market consolidation continues with strategic hires and acquisitions, while Fisher Investments charts a contrarian organic-growth path.

bullets.text: Wavvest poaches $1.4B advisor Vinnie Maliakkal from Schwab to bolster its AI-native platform, signaling competitive pressure on custody incumbents.
Choreo closes $700M RIA acquisition under new CEO Jason Van de Loo, continuing consolidation momentum in planning-focused wealth management.
Fisher Investments hits ~$450B AUM organically since 2019 (up from $112B), resisting M&A while competitors consolidate and adding $88B+ in just 18 months through media-driven marketing.
Goldman's RIA custody play gains traction with UHNW focus but remains a niche player in a market dominated by Schwab, Fidelity, and E*TRADE.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Specialty insurance and reinsurance consolidation accelerates across US, Asia, and Europe amid capacity expansion and geopolitical risks.

bullets.text: Gallagher Re's ceded reinsurance premiums jumped 18% YoY in 2025, fueled by surging US MGA market demand—a clear signal of sustained capacity needs in specialty lines.
RGA locked down two block coinsurance deals in Japan (Sumitomo Life, Anshin Life), cementing its Asian reinsurance expansion amid regional growth opportunities.
Qatari LNG tanker hit by projectile off Oman with two additional tankers targeted by Iran; shipping disruption and claims exposure mounting as Hormuz tensions escalate.
Nirvana acquiring Ryan Specialty's Swedish subsidiary assets and Acrisure launching UK private office for HNW clients—specialty insurance consolidation and service expansion spreading globally.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: 5 stories spanning payments infrastructure M&A, cross-border credit expansion, insurance portfolio moves, and regulatory tech mandates.

bullets.text: JPMorgan and BofA in early-stage talks to acquire Fiserv's debit network; no valuation or timeline yet, but signals consolidation appetite in payments infrastructure.
Equifax paying $750M for Círculo de Crédito to enter Mexico's high-growth credit bureau market, betting on emerging-market credit data expansion.
Carlyle's Fortitude Re acquiring $3.8B long-term care portfolio from Unum—alternative capital moving into recurring insurance fee revenue.
ECB requiring banks to file AI cyber-threat mitigation plans, signaling regulatory focus on advanced AI model risks to banking infrastructure.

PE & Deals.lede: Major allocators reshuffling: Novo Holdings pivots to GP solutions, Rest expands PE exposure, Temasek rebalancing away from EMEA underperformance, while GPs chase retail capital and secondaries remain fragmented.

bullets.text: Novo Holdings ($36bn AUM) exploring dedicated PE GP allocation and Asia expansion—signaling mega-LP appetite for manager solutions beyond traditional fund-of-funds.

Temasek admitting EMEA direct investment lag; pivoting 2031 strategy toward AI, private credit, and core-plus infrastructure—major reallocation signal from Singapore's $1.3tn+ sovereign wealth behemoth.
Patria closes $677m secondaries fund targeting smaller deals as market grapples with persistent DPI issues—smaller GPs doubling down on niche positioning.

Retail capital emerging as PE's new frontier, but structural and regulatory constraints limit deployment—Azalea's evergreen launch signals LP appetite, though channel remains structurally constrained.

Markets & Macro.lede: UniCredit makes aggressive move on Commerzbank; UK capital fleeing IPOs for M&A; credit markets decoupling from fundamentals as flows dominate.

bullets.text: UniCredit secures 48% of Commerzbank after tender offer, positioning for majority control of Germany's #2 bank despite political pushback from Berlin.
UK M&A bids hit £60bn vs. £2.2bn in new IPOs (27:1 ratio)—capital systematically rotating from primary markets into consolidation and secondary buyouts.
Credit markets showing Newton's Cradle effect: passive flows and algos driving momentum trades decoupled from underlying credit quality and fundamentals.
UK pension funds rotating out of gilts into AI-linked bonds as BoE holds steady, creating new asset class driven by narrative risk rather than traditional credit metrics.

Newsletters.lede: 5 stories spanning payments arbitrage, tax rule shifts favoring HNW clients, wealth concentration tailwinds, energy price pressure, and single-stock ETF proliferation.

bullets.text: Wall Street banks exploring card payment network acquisitions to dodge interchange fee caps that have drained billions in issuer revenue.
IRS reclassifies 'Trump Account' contributions as current-interest gifts, lowering gift-tax exposure and expanding HNW planning toolkit.
Wealth concentration accelerating across all income tiers per UBS report, fueling advisor hiring and AUM growth in wealth management.
Saudi Arabia slashing crude prices as Brent trades near $72/bbl—lowest since pre-war—signaling sector pricing power erosion.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: Five stories spanning sports tech tradeoffs, prediction market efficiency, historical land reclamation decline, and AI governance frameworks—plus a Pulitzer Prize author Q&A.

bullets.text: Prediction markets proved their edge: Maine Democratic primary dropout odds jumped from 9% to 96% in one day on scandal news, showing real-time information aggregation that beats traditional forecasting.

US coastal cities reclaimed 8% of land from water in 1890s–1920s but stopped after WWII despite real estate constraints—a signal of either waning engineering ambition or tighter regulation worth understanding.

AI governance raises constitutional legitimacy questions: applying consent theory to rule sets like Claude's Constitution matters operationally as financial institutions embed AI into critical decisions.

all.lede: Five stories spanning fintech charter ambitions, mega payments M&A, a hostile German bank consolidation play, and structural market mechanics.

bullets.text: Klarna applies for ILC charter with FDIC, signaling fintech's pivot to regulated deposit-taking and lending—a significant regulatory milestone.
JPMorgan and BofA exploring acquisition of Fiserv's debit network; banks seeking to escape interchange fee caps that have cost issuers tens of billions.
UniCredit secures 48% stake in Commerzbank via tender offer, positioning for majority control of Germany's second-largest bank despite political opposition from Berlin.
Technical factors (passive flows, algos, vol suppression) have decoupled credit from fundamentals, creating momentum-driven price discovery rather than credit-driven valuations.
